session_summary:
Completed a comprehensive feature parity and UX audit of the OpenCode Desktop application. The learning wave deployed 4 parallel subagents exploring the Electron shell (packages/desktop/), App renderer (packages/app/), TUI CLI (packages/opencode/), and UI library (packages/ui/). The critique wave synthesized all findings into a structured audit artifact at docs/json/opencode/artifacts/desktop-app-audit.v1.json.

Key discoveries:
- 21 feature parity gaps identified between TUI CLI and desktop app, with the most critical being: plugin management UI, GitHub/PR integration, MCP server management, agent configuration, subagent delegation, session export/import, and context compaction awareness
- 14 UX improvement opportunities found, highest impact being: onboarding flow, command palette expansion, session search, inline diff editing, progress visibility, keyboard shortcut discoverability
- 8 design system concerns including two parallel design systems (55 v1 + 26 v2 components) with no migration plan, no a11y testing, no visual regression testing
- 3 blockers discovered including a blocking IPC bug (installCli channel has no handler)
- 9 architectural observations about provider-hell patterns, sidecar single point of failure, Postgres migration opacity
- 3-phase roadmap proposed: 7 quick wins → 8 core parity → 8 UX leadership items

The desktop app has a strong foundation: SolidJS with @solidjs/router, 81 UI components across two design systems, 35 dynamic themes in Oklch color space, virtualized message timeline, rich composer, multi-tab terminal, session viz, and comprehensive i18n (18 languages). It's architecturally sound using Electron 41 with utilityProcess sidecar and 42 IPC channels. The path to feature parity and UX leadership is well-defined by the audit.
next_steps:
1. Fix the installCli orphaned IPC (blocking bug) — either add ipcMain.handle or remove the preload channel
2. Prioritize the 3-phase roadmap from the audit:
   - Phase 1 (Quick Wins): Session search, inline keyboard shortcut hints, MCP server browser UI, agent configuration UI, context compaction display, session export, settings polish
   - Phase 2 (Core Parity): Plugin marketplace UI, GitHub integration UI, PR publishing flow, full MCP management, subagent delegation in composer, stats dashboard
   - Phase 3 (UX Leadership): Onboarding flow redesign, command palette overhaul, inline diff editing, notification center, theme customizer, terminal with xterm.js
3. Consolidate the dual design system (v1 OC-2 → v2) with a written migration plan
4. Add automated a11y testing (axe-core) and visual regression testing (Chromatic/Percy)
5. Create formal design token documentation in docs/schemas/
6. Add migration state awareness to desktop UI for the Postgres backend transition

findings.summary: Desktop Electron shell uses Electron 41.2 with utilityProcess sidecar architecture, 3-tier IPC, comprehensive platform integration (macOS/Windows/Linux/WSL)
Desktop renderer uses SolidJS (not React) with @opencode-ai/ui component library (v1+v2), communicates via @opencode-ai/sdk/v2 to local server
TUI CLI offers 23+ commands (run, generate, providers, agent, session, models, mcp, github, pr, export, import, debug, stats, db, plugin, etc.)
Desktop app has rich UI with custom titlebar, virtualized timeline, rich prompt input with @mentions and /commands, terminal panel, session viz, multi-dialog settings
installCli IPC channel exposed in preload but no ipcMain.handle registered — renders calls from renderer
Desktop Electron shell: Electron 41.2, utilityProcess sidecar, 42 IPC channels, 3-tier architecture (Renderer→IPC→Main→Sidecar)
Desktop renderer: SolidJS SPA with 13 context providers, 300+ components, rich features including message timeline, composer, terminal, session viz, multi-dialog settings
TUI CLI: 23+ commands (run, serve, web, acp, mcp, session, providers, agent, models, github, pr, export, import, debug, db, plugin, etc.), 50+ built-in tools, Effect-TS architecture
UI library: 55 v1 + 26 v2 components, 35 dynamic themes (Oklch color space), @kobalte/core primitives, two parallel design systems with active migration
Key TUI features missing from desktop: plugin management UI, GitHub/PR integration UI, MCP management UI, debug tools panel, export/import UI, agent configuration UI, DB/admin tools
Desktop UX opportunities: onboarding flow, session visualization polish, inline diff editing, session comparison/diff, message search/filter, command palette improvements, file watch visualization
Two parallel design systems (v1 OC-2 and v2) with different token names coexist without clear migration plan; no a11y test automation; 2 components without story files
installCli IPC channel exposed in preload bridge but no ipcMain.handle registered — will hang renderer calls
21 feature parity gaps identified: plugin mgmt, GitHub/PR integration, MCP mgmt, agent config, subagent delegation, export/import, stats, context compaction are most critical missing features
14 UX improvement opportunities: onboarding flow, command palette, session search, inline diff editing, progress visibility, keyboard shortcut discoverability are highest impact
8 design system concerns: two parallel design systems (v1/v2), no a11y test automation, no visual regression testing, no formal token docs
3 blockers found: installCli orphaned IPC channel (blocking), dual design system debt (major), updater stderr pollution (minor)
3-phase roadmap recommended: 7 quick wins → 8 core parity → 8 UX leadership items
